data.title: U.S. February Unemployment Rate Rises Slightly
data.description: According to BlockBeats, the United States reported an unemployment rate of 4.1% for February, slightly higher than the anticipated 4.00% and the previous month's figure of 4.00%.

data.title: Federal Reserve's March Rate Decision Likely To Remain Unchanged
data.description: According to Odaily, CME's 'FedWatch' tool indicates a 91% probability that the Federal Reserve will maintain its current interest rates in March. There is a 9% chance of a 25 basis point rate cut. Looking ahead to May, there is a 53.8% likelihood that rates will remain unchanged, a 42.5% probability of a cumulative 25 basis point cut, and a 3.7% chance of a cumulative 50 basis point reduction.

data.title: U.S. Nonfarm Payrolls Miss Expectations, Signaling Potential Softening in Labor Market
data.description: Key Takeaways:February Nonfarm Payrolls (NFP) came in at 151,000, below expectations of 160,000.The previous month’s NFP was revised downward from 143,000 to 125,000, indicating weaker-than-reported job growth.The report suggests a slight cooling in the labor market, which could increase the likelihood of future Federal Reserve rate cuts.Labor Market Shows Signs of CoolingOn March 7, the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) reported that seasonally adjusted Nonfarm Payrolls (NFP) for February stood at 151,000, missing the forecast of 160,000.Additionally, January’s NFP was revised downward from 143,000 to 125,000, reinforcing a slower pace of job growth.Key Implication: A weaker-than-expected jobs report could fuel expectations for earlier Federal Reserve rate cuts, as the labor market softens.Market and Federal Reserve ImplicationsIncreased Rate Cut ProbabilityWith job growth slowing, the Fed may lean toward cutting rates sooner to prevent a further economic slowdown.The March Fed meeting (March 19-20) will be closely watched for any shift in tone on monetary easing.Bond Market ReactionLower-than-expected payrolls could push Treasury yields lower, as investors price in a more dovish Fed stance.Impact on Bitcoin and Crypto MarketsCrypto markets typically react positively to expectations of lower interest rates, as liquidity conditions ease.A weaker labor market could reinforce Bitcoin’s long-term appeal as a hedge against monetary expansion.What’s Next?March 12: U.S. CPI Inflation Report, a key data point that will influence Fed policy.March 19-20: Federal Reserve Meeting, where policymakers may signal future rate cut plans.Further labor market data to assess whether the cooling trend continues.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Surpasses 90,000 USDT with a Narrowed 0.36% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Mar 07, 2025, 13:37 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has crossed the 90,000 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 90,009.929688 USDT, with a narrowed narrowed 0.36% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Greeks.live: The market is too fragile, ETH has fallen 43% in the past year
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Greeks.live macro researcher Adam released a Chinese community briefing saying that market sentiment is generally bearish, and believes that even with the positive news of Trump signing an executive order, it is difficult to boost the market. Group members observed that the market is too fragile, and ETH is particularly weak, having fallen 43% in the past 365 days. After Trump signed the cryptocurrency executive order, the market fell instead of rising, and traders are optimistic about short-selling strategies in this environment.

data.title: JPMorgan Analysts Predict S&P 500 Fluctuations Amid Market Uncertainty
data.description: According to BlockBeats, on March 7, JPMorgan analysts forecast that the S&P 500 index will experience fluctuations between 5200 and 6000 points in the short term. This prediction is attributed to ongoing market volatility, policy uncertainty, and investor position risks. Despite these challenges, JPMorgan maintains a year-end target of 6500 points for the index, acknowledging a significant margin of error around this forecast. Analysts suggest that the S&P 500 may not reach this level until 2026. The market is expected to regain strength later this year, driven by potential policy shifts under U.S. President Donald Trump's administration and continued productivity gains from artificial intelligence advancements.

data.title: Safe Wallet Services Fully Restored After Security Incident
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Safe has announced that its Safe{Wallet} services have resumed normal operations as of March 7. All networks and Safe API are now fully repaired and available for use. Previously, Safe had temporarily suspended some functions and conducted a comprehensive security review following a hacking incident involving ByBit, where Safe serves as a multi-signature wallet service provider.

data.title: Before the release of the non-farm payrolls, the probability that the Fed will keep interest rates unchanged in March is 91%
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to CME's "Fed Watch": The probability of the Fed keeping interest rates unchanged in March is 91%, and the probability of a 25 basis point rate cut is 9%. The probability of maintaining the current interest rate unchanged by May is 53.8%, the probability of a cumulative 25 basis point rate cut is 42.5%, and the probability of a cumulative 50 basis point rate cut is 3.7%. (Jinshi)

data.title: Goldman Sachs Reports Increased U.S. Recession Risk
data.description: According to PANews, recent data from Goldman Sachs indicates that the risk of a U.S. economic recession has risen from 14% in January to 23%. This increase highlights growing concerns about the economic outlook.

data.title: BitFuFu: 219 BTC mined in February, currently holding 1,800 BTC
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily reported that BitFuFu released its February operational update data, which disclosed that its mining output in February was 219 BTC (including 58 BTC of self-mining output and 161 BTC of cloud mining output), and its current bitcoin holdings are 1,800.
